description: Source table or figure used as evidence.
Material format, such as bulk gel, coating, bead, cryogel, or nanogel.
Post-treatment, washing, conditioning, or storage notes.
Degradation, reuse, storage, or stability notes.
Role of the protein in the experiment.
Reported or inferred protein charge at assay pH.
Matrix, such as buffer, serum, plasma, or crude extract.
Batch, surface, column, SPR/QCM, or other assay mode.
Endpoint, equilibrium, kinetic, dynamic, or unknown.
Single protein, binary, serum, plasma, blood, or other competition context.
Normalized capacity in mg/g if extractable.
Normalized capacity in mg/mL bed if extractable.
Surface adsorption in microgram per square centimeter.
Dynamic binding capacity as reported, including its original units when available.
Selectivity factor as reported for competitive or imprinted systems.
Imprinting factor for molecularly imprinted hydrogel systems.
Association constant as reported, including its original units when available.
Dissociation constant as reported, including its original units when available.
Mechanism tags extracted from the article.
Whether the outcome value was normalized.
Whether the record should be manually reviewed.

Controlled interaction class: physical adsorption, nonspecific adsorption, selective adsorption, affinity binding, covalent immobilization, entrapment, antifouling low adsorption, protein fouling, or unknown.
yes/no/unclear flag indicating whether protein binding or immobilization is covalent.
Coarse controlled assay mode derived from experiment_mode, adsorption_type, detection method, outcome, and evidence.
Fine controlled assay mode derived from the same fields while preserving the original experiment_mode text.
Controlled application context: biointerface adsorption, purification, antifouling, protein immobilization, hemocompatibility, screening library, mechanism study, or other.
Comparability tier for the selected target: strong, medium, weak, or not_comparable.
Curated model-ready flag using standardized interaction/application/comparability fields.
Semicolon-separated reasons preventing model_ready=true, or ready.
Rule-derived manual review priority: high, medium, or low.
Whether an antifouling/low-adsorption candidate has protein, serum, plasma, albumin, fibrinogen, IgG, or total-protein evidence.
Q5 protein-antifouling rule action: keep, downweight, exclude_candidate, or not_applicable.
Version identifier for the deterministic standardization rules.
